regions.name: North Africa
regions.subtitle: The Maghreb Ritual
regions.description: In Morocco, Tunisia, and Algeria, tea is not a beverage — it is an act of hospitality. Poured from great height into ornate glasses, Maghreb mint tea is a symbol of friendship, negotiation, and welcome.

culturalPairing.ritual: The three glasses tradition — the first is gentle as life, the second strong as love, the third bitter as death.
culturalPairing.music: Gnawa rhythms and Andalusian classical melodies

culturalPairing.ritual: Brewed in a small pot over coals, served in three rounds of increasing sweetness.
culturalPairing.music: Tinariwen — Desert blues and Tuareg guitar
culturalPairing.literature: Ibrahim al-Koni — The Bleeding of the Stone

regions.name: East Asia
regions.subtitle: The Way of Tea
regions.description: From Japan's meditative Chanoyu ceremony to China's ancient Gongfu tradition, East Asian tea culture is a philosophy — a practice of presence, respect, and impermanence.

culturalPairing.ritual: Chanoyu — the Japanese tea ceremony rooted in Zen Buddhism. Every movement is intentional.
culturalPairing.literature: Kakuzo Okakura — The Book of Tea

culturalPairing.ritual: Gongfu Cha — a meditative multi-steep technique using a tiny Yixing clay pot.
culturalPairing.literature: Lu Yu — The Classic of Tea (760 AD)

regions.name: South America
regions.subtitle: The Circle of Yerba
regions.description: In Argentina, Uruguay, and southern Brazil, sharing mate from a single gourd is a ritual of equality and trust — a circle where everyone drinks from the same vessel.

culturalPairing.ritual: The cebador prepares and serves. Saying 'gracias' means you're done. The gourd passes left.
culturalPairing.music: Chamame folk music of the Litoral region

culturalPairing.ritual: Shared among gauchos on the pampas, prepared fresh with a cuia and bomba.
culturalPairing.music: Milonga — the folk music of the southern plains
culturalPairing.literature: Érico Veríssimo — O Tempo e o Vento

regions.name: South Asia
regions.subtitle: Chai as Communion
regions.description: From the bustling chaiwallahs of Mumbai to the misty plantations of Darjeeling, tea in South Asia is woven into the rhythm of daily life — every street corner, every conversation.

culturalPairing.ritual: Simmered slowly with cardamom, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves. Served in small clay cups — kulhads.
culturalPairing.music: Ravi Shankar — Sitar ragas at dusk
culturalPairing.literature: Arundhati Roy — The God of Small Things

culturalPairing.ritual: Known as the 'Champagne of Teas,' best enjoyed without milk to appreciate its muscatel notes.
culturalPairing.music: Sarod melodies — the reflective tones of the hills
culturalPairing.literature: Ruskin Bond — A Town Called Dehra
